nativeRange.habitat: Reef slopes, reef flats, and sandy lagoon bottoms in shallow tropical reef zones; typically rests free on sandy substrate rather than attached to rock

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via zooxanthellae. Benefits from target-feeding meaty foods — micro-plankton, brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, or small meaty pieces — roughly once weekly. Tentacles are generally extended during daylight and can actively capture zooplankton.

avoidWith: other corals placed within tentacle reach (8-15 cm)
Fungia sp. (short tentacle plate corals) — may compete for same sand-bed territory

summary: Heliofungia actiniformis is a free-living LPS coral whose long tentacles (up to ~15 cm) closely resemble a sea anemone. Generally suited for intermediate reefers; requires a sand bed, moderate light, and low flow.
careNotes:
Heliofungia actiniformis is distinctive among Fungiidae for its unusually long tentacles — generally reported at 8–15 cm when extended — which give the coral a strong visual resemblance to a large sea anemone. It is sometimes confused with anemones by beginning hobbyists. Unlike most stony corals, it is free-living (not attached to substrate) and should be placed directly on a sandy bottom rather than on live rock. It can move slowly across the sand bed over time by inflating and deflating its body.

Key care points:
• Placement: Sandy substrate in the lower third of the aquarium. The coral needs clear space in all directions — sweeper tentacles can extend well beyond its visible disc and will sting nearby corals. Allow at least 15–20 cm of clear space around the specimen.
• Lighting: Moderate; generally advised to keep under 75–150 PAR. High-intensity direct lighting can bleach the colony. Acclimate gradually if moving from a lower-light environment.
• Flow: Low to gentle indirect flow is preferred. Strong direct flow will cause the coral to retract and may injure tissue. Gentle random tumbling flow (not laminar) is suitable.
• Feeding: Has been reported to respond well to weekly target-feeding of meaty foods (brine shrimp, mysis, chopped seafood) delivered directly to the tentacles when extended. Zooxanthellae provide a substantial portion of metabolic needs under adequate light.
• Aggression: The long sweeper tentacles have a potent sting and are nearly always extended, making this coral a consistent threat to nearby corals. It is commonly considered one of the more aggressive LPS corals for this reason, despite its anemone-like appearance suggesting passivity.
• Water chemistry: Standard reef parameters are generally sufficient. Supplemental calcium, strontium, and trace elements support skeletal growth. Sudden changes in alkalinity or salinity tend to cause rapid tissue recession.
• Distinct from sibling genus: Heliofungia (one species: H. actiniformis) has significantly longer, knob-tipped tentacles compared to Fungia sp. (short tentacle plate corals). The two genera are sometimes sold interchangeably in the trade but have different care profiles — Heliofungia generally prefers lower flow and lower light than many Fungia species.
• Cross-source note: LiveAquaria lists care level as 'Moderate' and flow as 'Medium'; controller specification and broader community consensus indicate 'low' flow is more accurate for this species' preference. Temperature conversion from LiveAquaria 72–78°F yields 22.2–25.6°C; rounded to 22–26°C. Ca/Mg PPM ranges not stated by primary source; standard LPS reef targets applied (Ca 380–450, Mg 1250–1400). Only two sources could be successfully consulted — all other secondaries (BRS, WWC, Reef2Reef, AquaticCommunity, TFH, numerous specialty sites) returned 404, 403, ECONNREFUSED, or timeout errors.
breedingNotes: Sexual reproduction via broadcast spawning on natural reefs. Captive breeding has been researched in laboratory settings using larvae rearing, but this is not practiced in the home aquarium hobby. Fragging is not straightforward for free-living Fungiidae compared to branching corals; the species is typically propagated by splitting naturally or via controlled aquaculture facilities. Not commonly bred in captivity.

primary.name: LiveAquaria – Plate Coral, Long Tentacle
primary.notes: Provided water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025, dKH 8–12), care level (Moderate), lighting (Moderate), flow (Medium), placement (Bottom), temperament (Aggressive), and diet (micro-plankton or brine shrimp). Did not provide Ca/Mg PPM or adult size in cm.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Heliofungia actiniformis
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Family Fungiidae, Order Scleractinia), native range (eastern Indian Ocean, central Indo-Pacific, Australia, Japan, South China Sea, West Pacific islands), depth range (1–25 m), adult size (disc up to 20 cm diameter, 7 cm height), and confirming zooxanthellate + zooplankton predator feeding strategy. Not used for aquarium husbandry parameters.
